agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a critical lifecycle event in logistics and courier operations. A cancellation reverses a customer's intent to ship, but the feasibility and cost of cancellation depend entirely on how far the order has progressed through the fulfillment pipeline. An order that hasn't been dispatched is trivially cancellable; an order already in transit requires physical recall, rerouting, or return — each with real-world cost. The PM spec is dangerously vague: it says nothing about lifecycle stage, refund policy, partial fulfillment, or who bears the cost.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A customer's request to pick up and deliver a package from origin to destination, tracked through its full lifecycle."},{"term":"Order Status","definition":"The current lifecycle stage of an order. Typical statuses: PLACED, CONFIRMED, ASSIGNED, PICKED_UP, IN_TRANSIT, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, DELIVERED, CANCELLED, FAILED."},{"term":"Cancellation","definition":"A customer- or system-initiated request to terminate an order before successful delivery. Results in the order transitioning to CANCELLED status."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The set of order statuses during which cancellation is permitted. Once an order exits this window, cancellation is no longer possible or incurs penalties."},{"term":"Cancellation Fee","definition":"A charge applied when cancellation occurs after resources (courier, vehicle, fuel) have already been committed to the order."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of payment to the customer upon cancellation. May be full or partial depending on the cancellation stage and applicable fees."},{"term":"Courier","definition":"The person or agent assigned to physically fulfill the pickup and delivery of an order."},{"term":"Dispatch","definition":"The act of assigning a courier to an order and committing operational resources to fulfill it."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An order may only be cancelled if its current status is within the cancellation window (PLACED, CONFIRMED, or ASSIGNED).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing cancellation of a DELIVERED or IN_TRANSIT order would corrupt order state and create physical-world inconsistencies (package already moved, courier already en route). The system cannot 'undo' a physical pickup.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with status IN_TRANSIT, PICKED_UP, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, or DELIVERED, when a cancellation is requested, then the system MUST reject the request and return an error indicating the order is no longer cancellable."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A cancelled order MUST transition to CANCELLED status and no further status transitions are permitted from CANCELLED.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"CANCELLED is a terminal state. Allowing transitions out of CANCELLED would corrupt audit trails, break billing reconciliation, and violate the order lifecycle state machine.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in CANCELLED status, when any status transition is attempted, then the system MUST reject the transition."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"If a courier has been assigned (status = ASSIGNED) but has not yet picked up the package, cancellation is permitted but a cancellation fee MAY apply.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Whether a fee applies and its amount is a business policy decision that operations or finance can adjust. The rule that cancellation is allowed at this stage is firm, but the fee is policy.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with status ASSIGNED, when the customer cancels, then the system MUST allow cancellation AND calculate any applicable cancellation fee per current policy."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Only the customer who placed the order (the order owner) may initiate a cancellation. System admins may cancel on behalf of a customer with an override reason.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing arbitrary users to cancel others' orders is a data integrity and authorization violation. This is a fundamental access control invariant.","testable_assertion":"Given a cancellation request from a user who is NOT the order owner and does NOT have admin override privileges, the system MUST reject the request with an authorization error."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A cancellation MUST trigger a refund. The refund amount depends on the order status at the time of cancellation: full refund if PLACED or CONFIRMED, refund minus cancellation fee if ASSIGNED.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund amounts and fee schedules are business policy. The requirement that SOME refund process is triggered is invariant, but the amounts are policy-driven.","testable_assertion":"Given a successful cancellation, then a refund record MUST be created with the correct amount based on the status at time of cancellation."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"When an order with status ASSIGNED is cancelled, the assigned courier MUST be notified and unassigned from the order.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Failing to notify and unassign the courier creates a physical-world inconsistency: the courier would attempt a pickup for a cancelled order, wasting resources and causing a poor experience.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with status ASSIGNED is cancelled, then the courier assignment MUST be removed AND a notification event MUST be emitted to the courier."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Every cancellation MUST record an audit entry capturing: who cancelled, when, the order status at time of cancellation, the reason (if provided), and the refund amount.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Audit trail integrity is non-negotiable for financial reconciliation, dispute resolution, and regulatory compliance.","testable_assertion":"Given a successful cancellation, then an audit record MUST exist containing actor_id, timestamp, prior_status, cancellation_reason, and refund_amount."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An order exists with status PLACED or CONFIRMED","when":"The order owner requests cancellation","then":"The order transitions to CANCELLED, a full refund is initiated, and an audit record is created"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An order exists with status ASSIGNED and a courier is assigned","when":"The order owner requests cancellation","then":"The order transitions to CANCELLED, the courier is unassigned and notified, a refund minus any applicable cancellation fee is initiated, and an audit record is created"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An order exists with status PICKED_UP, IN_TRANSIT, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, or DELIVERED","when":"The order owner requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation request is rejected with a clear error message indicating the order can no longer be cancelled, and the order status remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An order exists with status PLACED","when":"A user who is NOT the order owner and does NOT have admin privileges requests cancellation","then":"The request is rejected with an authorization error and the order status remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An order has been successfully cancelled","when":"Any further status transition is attempted on that order","then":"The transition is rejected and the order remains in CANCELLED status"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An order exists with status CONFIRMED","when":"The order owner requests cancellation and provides a reason","then":"The cancellation reason is persisted in the audit record alongside actor, timestamp, prior status, and refund amount"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An order exists with status ASSIGNED","when":"A system admin cancels the order on behalf of the customer with an override reason","then":"The order transitions to CANCELLED, the admin identity and override reason are captured in the audit record, and the courier is unassigned and notified"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An order exists with status CANCELLED","when":"The order owner attempts to cancel it again","then":"The system returns an idempotent response indicating the order is already cancelled, with no duplicate refund or audit entry created"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Simultaneous cancellation and courier pickup: the customer requests cancellation at the exact moment the courier marks the order as PICKED_UP","expected_behavior":"The system must use optimistic locking or a compare-and-swap on order status. Whichever transition commits first wins. If pickup commits first, cancellation is rejected. If cancellation commits first, the courier's pickup attempt is rejected and the courier is notified."},{"scenario":"Double cancellation: the customer clicks 'cancel' twice rapidly or retries after a timeout","expected_behavior":"The cancellation operation must be idempotent. The second request returns success (already cancelled) without creating a duplicate refund or audit entry."},{"scenario":"Cancellation during payment processing: the original payment is still being authorized when cancellation is requested","expected_behavior":"The system should cancel/void the pending payment authorization rather than charging and then refunding. If charge already settled, a standard refund is issued."},{"scenario":"Network failure after cancellation committed but before courier notification sent","expected_behavior":"Courier notification must be retried with at-least-once delivery semantics. The order remains CANCELLED regardless of notification delivery status. A dead-letter or retry mechanism must ensure the courier is eventually notified."},{"scenario":"Partial fulfillment: a multi-package order where some packages are picked up and others are not","expected_behavior":"This spec does not address partial cancellation. If multi-package orders exist, the system should reject full cancellation if any package is already picked up, OR support partial cancellation with prorated refund. This is an ambiguity that must be resolved."},{"scenario":"Cancellation of an order with a promotional discount or coupon applied","expected_behavior":"The refund must reflect the actual amount charged (post-discount), not the list price. Any single-use coupon should be restored to the customer's account if business policy permits."},{"scenario":"Cancellation fee exceeds the refund amount","expected_behavior":"The cancellation fee must never result in a negative refund. If the fee equals or exceeds the order value, the refund is zero and the customer is clearly informed before confirming cancellation."},{"scenario":"Order placed by a business account where the requester is an employee, not the account owner","expected_behavior":"Authorization rules for business accounts must define whether any employee on the account can cancel, or only the specific placer. This is an ambiguity to resolve with the PM."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellation directly impacts revenue (reversed transactions), courier compensation (wasted dispatch), and refund processing costs. A well-implemented cancellation window minimizes revenue leakage from late cancellations while maintaining customer trust. Cancellation fees for ASSIGNED orders partially recover sunk dispatch costs.","operational":"Every cancellation after courier assignment wastes dispatch capacity. High cancellation rates at the ASSIGNED stage signal a UX or pricing problem. The courier notification pipeline must be reliable — a missed notification means a wasted trip. Audit trail completeness is critical for dispute resolution and financial reconciliation.","user":"Customers expect frictionless cancellation for orders not yet in motion. Blocking cancellation too early frustrates users; allowing it too late creates operational chaos. Clear communication about the cancellation window and any applicable fees builds trust. An idempotent, responsive cancellation flow reduces support tickets.","risk":"Race conditions between cancellation and pickup are the highest-risk technical scenario — if both succeed, the system is in a corrupt state. Lack of idempotency risks duplicate refunds (direct financial loss). Missing audit trails create compliance and dispute-resolution gaps. Authorization gaps could allow malicious cancellation of others' orders.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Gain ability to cancel unwanted orders and receive refunds. Must understand cancellation window and fee structure clearly to avoid frustration."},{"group":"Couriers","impact":"Must receive timely cancellation notifications to avoid wasted pickups. Late or missing notifications directly waste their time and erode trust in the platform."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Must reconcile refunds, cancellation fees, and voided payments. Audit completeness is critical for month-end close and dispute resolution."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will handle edge cases: disputed cancellation fees, orders stuck in ambiguous states, missed courier notifications. Clear status and audit trails reduce support burden."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Cancellations after assignment reduce courier utilization. Need visibility into cancellation rates by stage to optimize dispatch timing."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The PM spec is a single sentence with no mention of lifecycle stages, refund policy, fee structure, authorization model, multi-package orders, business accounts, or concurrency behavior. Almost every business rule and acceptance criterion required domain inference and assumptions. Several edge cases reveal genuine ambiguities that cannot be resolved without PM clarification. The spec quality is very low.","high_confidence_areas":["CANCELLED is a terminal state — this is a universal logistics invariant","Cancellation must be restricted by lifecycle stage — you cannot cancel a delivered order","Authorization must be enforced — only the order owner can cancel","Audit trail is required for financial and compliance reasons"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact cancellation window boundaries (is ASSIGNED cancellable? What about a 'DISPATCHED' sub-state?)","Cancellation fee amounts and policy","Multi-package / partial cancellation behavior","Business account authorization model","Coupon/promo restoration policy","Whether the customer must confirm cancellation after seeing the fee"],"assumptions":["The order lifecycle includes at minimum: PLACED, CONFIRMED, ASSIGNED, PICKED_UP, IN_TRANSIT, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, DELIVERED, CANCELLED","Cancellation window includes PLACED, CONFIRMED, and ASSIGNED but not beyond","A cancellation fee may apply at the ASSIGNED stage — this is assumed, not specified","Refunds are full for pre-assignment cancellations — assumed, not specified","Orders are single-package unless otherwise stated","Payment has been captured or authorized at time of cancellation"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define which order statuses are cancellable. The cancellation window is the single most important business rule for this feature and it is entirely unspecified.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers","Operations"],"options":["Allow cancellation only for PLACED/CONFIRMED","Allow cancellation up to and including ASSIGNED","Allow cancellation up to PICKED_UP with escalating fees"],"recommendation":"Allow cancellation for PLACED, CONFIRMED, and ASSIGNED. ASSIGNED incurs a cancellation fee. Beyond ASSIGNED, cancellation is blocked. This balances customer flexibility with operational cost recovery."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define refund policy or cancellation fee structure. Without this, we cannot implement the financial side of cancellation.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance / Billing"],"options":["Full refund at all cancellable stages","Full refund pre-assignment, partial refund post-assignment","No refund after confirmation"],"recommendation":"Full refund for PLACED/CONFIRMED, refund minus a configurable cancellation fee for ASSIGNED. Fee should be a percentage or flat amount configurable by operations."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-package orders and partial cancellation are not addressed. If the platform supports multi-package orders, the cancellation behavior is undefined.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Operations","Finance / Billing"],"options":["Block cancellation if any package is past the window","Allow partial cancellation with prorated refund","Treat each package as an independent cancellable unit"],"recommendation":"Clarify with PM whether multi-package orders exist in the current system. If yes, define partial cancellation rules before implementation."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Business account authorization model is unknown. For B2B customers, it is unclear whether any employee on the account can cancel any order, or only the original placer.","affected_stakeholders":["Business account customers","Customer Support"],"options":["Only original placer can cancel","Any user on the business account can cancel","Role-based: account admins can cancel any order, regular users only their own"],"recommendation":"Implement role-based cancellation for business accounts: account admins can cancel any order, individual users can cancel only orders they placed."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a critical lifecycle event in logistics and courier operations.
